verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a cytosolic chaperone with documented, stress-induced surface presentation in tumor and heat-shocked cells. The protein reaches the outer leaflet via direct insertion into the plasma membrane through high-affinity binding to phosphatidylserine (PS) and phosphoinositides (PIPs)—its own lipid-anchoring mechanism, not transient recruitment to other surface proteins. Surface localization is wash-resistant and functionally significant (associated with radiation resistance, invasion, and immune recognition). This represents cell-state-induced (stress + oncogenic transformation) ecto-display, a canonical `contextual` category.
